message:
chore(beads): close ft-sz7lc

Sync close transition for dogfood captured_at canonicalization in e4719ebc.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-4o5mb","title":"[conformance] wa.search / wa.get_text / wa.send / wa.wait_for lack schema+envelope golden roundtrip","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance.rs` pins envelope + schema conformance for exactly ONE tool: `wa.rules_list` (tests at lines 237-294, 313-346). `conformance_mcp_manifest.rs` pins the MANIFEST (tool schemas, additionalProperties=false, resource templates) but does NOT exercise the per-tool request → response envelope roundtrip.

`grep -oE '"wa\.[a-z_]+"' crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance.rs | sort -u` → only `"wa.rules_list"`.

Out of 29 advertised `wa.*` tools in `tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`, 28 have zero call-and-assert-envelope coverage in the conformance suite. The four core operator-facing tools are:

* `wa.search` — JSON envelope shape, hit schema, TOON parity, pane_filter/since_filter/until_filter/mode/metrics fields
* `wa.get_text` — pane_text map shape (ok/error variants), truncated + truncation_info edges
* `wa.send` — sender/response envelope (distinct from get_text), error codes for denied sends
* `wa.wait_for` — matched/elapsed_ms/polls/is_regex fields, timeout response envelope, rule_id match path

## Shape of fix

Mirror the `mcp_conformance_rules_list_*` test pattern (lines 237-294 in mcp_conformance.rs):

1. spawn_client() → call_tool("wa.search", …)
2. parse_tool_envelope() → assert_success_envelope_shape() → assert_*_data_matches_documented_schema()
3. Also pin: invalid-format rejection, invalid-args hint text, TOON parity where applicable.

Existing helpers (`assert_object_matches_documented_schema`, `assert_common_envelope_fields`, `assert_success_envelope_shape`, `assert_invalid_args_envelope_shape`) already generalise — the new tests should reuse them verbatim.

## Why this matters

The MCP manifest conformance is the FREEZE on the advertised schema. Without per-tool envelope tests, a refactor can pass `conformance_mcp_manifest` (schema still looks right) while silently returning a non-conforming response body — e.g. field renamed in `ScannerHit` → clients break but tests stay green. ft-bs0ec froze this for `agent_inventory`; the four core operator tools deserve the same treatment.

## Scope guard

One new test file `mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs` (or additions to `mcp_conformance.rs`) with 4× (success + invalid-args) = 8 tests. ~250 LOC. No production changes.

## Explicitly out of scope

Mission / tx / events / cass families (separate bead if needed). Manifest tests (already covered). Resource templates (already covered).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:57.026144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:46:38.216319Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3608,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb","author":"jemanuel","text":"Blocker (pane3, 2026-04-23): fastmcp-rust enforces tool input_schema type validation at the JSON-RPC boundary — wrong-typed required fields (e.g. pane_id string where integer required) return McpError::InvalidParams from call_tool BEFORE reaching the per-tool handler's FT-MCP-0001 envelope path. The pattern mcp_conformance.rs:280 uses for wa.rules_list (optional string agent_type) cannot be replayed for wa.search/get_text/send/wait_for via wrong-typed-required inputs. Next-hop approach: pin the SUCCESS envelope shape via {timeout_secs:1, pane_id:999, pattern:'never-matches', format:'json'} for wait_for (times out in ~1s, returns Ok envelope with matched:false/elapsed_ms/polls). Draft tests left UNTRACKED at crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs — first pass covers invalid-args envelopes and doesn't work as noted. Bead stays in_progress for handoff.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:27:29Z"}]}

Narrow lane used: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-MistyCoast-target CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test wa_state_mcp_conformance --features mcp,asupersync-runtime. That fell back locally on RCH-E324, then local wrapper reproduced a compile-stop before the new test ran: crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs has non-exhaustive matches for Err(events::RecvError::Cancelled) at lines 574, 586, 749, and 786 (E0004). I stayed inside the assigned slice; only new file is crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs and it remains uncommitted pending baseline compile repair.","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:50:51Z"}]}

Moving to fallback bead wa-nu4.3.9.19 instead of contending on the same conformance file.","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:14:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-4qom2","title":"[SEV] tmux cmd_queue cap only enforced at pop site; unbounded growth when state != Idle","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue has CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000 enforced only inside send_next_command at line 289 via cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286-288 when state != Idle, so the drain-oldest logic never runs if tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse.

Four push sites append to cmd_queue with NO cap check:
- line 196: Event::LayoutChange -> ListAllPanes
- line 223: Event::SessionChanged -> ListCommands
- line 234: Event::WindowAdd -> ListAllWindows
- line 375: split_pane (user split) -> SplitPane

Concrete interleaving: (1) tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse; (2) tmux server is slow/hung (real-world scenario: remote tmux on lagging SSH link, tmux server overloaded, Event::Guarded response delayed); (3) GUI user resizes window repeatedly -> stream of Event::LayoutChange -> each push_back at line 196 runs without cap check; (4) send_next_command early-returns because state != Idle. Queue grows unbounded -> memory leak, eventual OOM.

The CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH comment at line 32-33 explicitly advertises 'prevent unbounded memory growth during event storms' as a safety invariant. That invariant is currently only upheld at the pop site, where it can't run under the exact condition (stuck tmux) that event storms exploit.

Fix: extract the cap-enforcement logic into a push_command_capped helper that applies the drain-oldest + warn/error logging at every push site. The helper runs under the cmd_queue lock (just acquired by the caller), so it's atomic with the push and has no additional locking cost.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:17:26.601099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.160715Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.158111Z","close_reason":"Completed: added push_command_capped() helper and routed all 4 production push sites (LayoutChange/SessionChanged/WindowAdd/split_pane) through it. The hard cap is now enforced at push time, not only at pop time — closing the unbounded-growth window when tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse. cargo check passes clean. Commit 1055cffc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4z7vh","title":"RobotCommands::Workflow builds PolicyEngine without require_prompt_active/command_gate/rules — bypasses operator safety config","description":"AUTH BYPASS (robot mode mutations, Severity: Medium).

crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs has 10 PolicyEngine::new call sites. 8 of them wire require_prompt_active from config + .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()). Two do NOT, both under RobotCommands::Workflow:

Site 1 (main.rs:19831, RobotCommands::Workflow::Run):

  let policy_engine = PolicyEngine::new(
      config.safety.rate_limit_per_pane,
      config.safety.rate_limit_global,
      false, // Don't require prompt active for robot mode
  )
  .with_tuning(&config.tuning);

No command_gate, no policy_rules, and require_prompt_active hardcoded false. This engine is fed into CxPolicyInjector → WorkflowRunner; any workflow step that calls send_text (e.g., handle_compaction sending '/compact', handle_usage_limit sending '/refresh') bypasses:
  - the operator's safety.require_prompt_active=true policy,
  - every [[safety.rules]] custom gate,
  - the [safety.command_gate] allow/deny list.

Site 2 (main.rs:20511, RobotCommands::Workflow::Abort): identical construction. abort_execution_with_cx runs compensation steps through the same injector; any send_text in a compensation path bypasses the same three surfaces.

Compare the correctly-gated paths:
  - RobotCommands::Send at main.rs:17652 uses config.safety.require_prompt_active AND .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules.
  - Non-robot workflow invocations at main.rs:21361, 21533, 25051, 27347 all wire the full policy surface.

NOT a duplicate of ft-48ah6 (read-only policy): this bead is specifically about the write/mutation surface (workflow's send_text / command-bearing steps). ft-48ah6 covered the read/search side.

Fix: replace the hardcoded 'false' with config.safety.require_prompt_active at both sites, and chain .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()) before passing the engine to PolicyGatedInjector. Rate-limit args are already correct.

Regression test: a send inside a robot-invoked workflow must be DENIED when config.safety.require_prompt_active=true AND no prompt is active, matching the direct-send gate behavior. Currently it is allowed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:46:59.925741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084786Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084480Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 834b59f2. Both RobotCommands::Workflow::Run (main.rs:19831) and ::Abort (main.rs:20520) now build PolicyEngine with config.safety.require_prompt_active and chain .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules — matching the construction at RobotCommands::Send (main.rs:17652) and non-robot workflow sites (21361/21533/25051/27347). Regression test robot_workflow_policy_engine_denies_send_when_prompt_required_ft_4z7vh asserts SendText is denied when require_prompt_active=true + command_running. Test binary compile is currently blocked by pre-existing concurrent-agent breakage (E0603 macro select private, E0063 missing defer_fts_triggers field) unrelated to this fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-55l4f","title":"[review][MEDIUM] wa_wait_for.json golden freezes polls:1 — may flake under high-concurrency CI load","description":"In crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_wait_for.json (landed in ecae6caa):

    "polls": 1

The canonicalize fn in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs only zeroes `now | elapsed_ms | captured_at | *_ms | score | semantic_score`. The `polls` field is not canonicalized.

Under load, this can flake:
- Each wa.wait_for poll spawns a fresh python3 subprocess (fake-wezterm.py) via tokio::Command in crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:2142-2155.
- On first-poll cache-cold with the swarm running 20+ agents hammering CPU/disk, the subprocess spawn + file read can be slow enough that the poll scheduler issues a second poll before the first returns matching text (build ready matches ready$ immediately on content).
- polls jumps to 2+, golden is polls==1, test fails.

Repro likelihood: low under bench, moderate-to-high under concurrent cargo test + rch + other agent builds hammering I/O.

Fix options (pick one):
1. Canonicalize `polls` to 0 in canonicalize() alongside elapsed_ms/now/captured_at. Pro: stable golden. Con: loses a genuine signal — golden no longer catches regressions where wait_for suddenly needs 10 polls for a match it used to catch on the first try.
2. Split `polls` out of the golden capture: keep it in success_envelope but canonicalize to "polls >= 1" assertion in assert_wait_for_success_data (already there at mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:~590 — `polls >= 1`), then canonicalize polls to 0 so the golden only sees the structural presence.
3. Accept the flake risk and document that re-running tests clears transient failures.

Option 2 is cleanest: the semantic "polls >= 1" invariant stays enforced; the byte-stable golden covers structure.

Severity: MEDIUM. Not a correctness bug; CI flake risk. Discovered during reviewer audit round 7, 2026-04-23 when verifying ecae6caa (ft-qyw1z+ft-4o5mb CRITICAL fix).

Tag: review, mcp, conformance, flake.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:30.203619Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:47:17.843645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:47:17.843379Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 05c8d36b (Option 2 from the bead). Semantic invariant polls>=1 remains enforced in assert_wait_for_success_data at mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:474-477 (runs against the live envelope at :733, BEFORE canonicalization). canonicalize() at :480 now zeroes polls alongside now/elapsed_ms/captured_at so the byte-stable golden only freezes structural presence, not scheduler-timing-dependent count. wa_wait_for.json updated to polls:0 to match. Only golden in the tree carrying a polls key is wa_wait_for.json, so the canonicalize rule is unambiguous and cannot mask a real regression in another tool's envelope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
@@ -283,7 +283,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-scoz7","title":"disk_pressure PressureThresholds::normalized() NaN slip-through (companion to ft-761tz threshold side)","description":"ft-761tz guarded usage_fraction.is_nan() in classify_tier but PressureThresholds::normalized() still used f64::clamp which preserves NaN. Misconfigured threshold (NaN yellow/red/black from permissive JSON/TOML parsers or buggy caller) produced Green for any usage including 1.0 — reinstating the exact ft-761tz bug on the config side. Fix landed in b0cce4da: snap NaN fields to Default before clamp. Regression test thresholds_with_nan_fields_fall_back_to_defaults_instead_of_green pinned 3 shapes (single-NaN-black, single-NaN-red, all-NaN).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:08:32.141295Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:23.215354Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:23.215167Z","close_reason":"Landed in b0cce4da (fix(disk_pressure): NaN thresholds fall back to Default in PressureThresholds::normalized)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-sl1xk","title":"shell_escape must not turn empty restore cwd into bare cd","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:31:45.320983Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:39:05.107651Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:39:05.107317Z","close_reason":"already landed in 3b525792","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-swa8k","title":"[SEV] workflows/runner.rs orphan-cx fallbacks break ambient-cx inheritance in 3 sites","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs lines 729, 1498, 2021 all use the stale fallback pattern:

  let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();
  let injector_cx = cx.unwrap_or(&request_cx);

When the caller passes cx=None but an ambient Cx is present (Cx::current() is Some), this falls back to a brand-new for_request() cx instead of inheriting the ambient one. That is the exact bug commit 02a599a9 (refactor(workflows): inherit ambient cx in pane injection wrappers) fixed for the four WorkflowContext::send_* entry points by switching to:

  let cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);

Concrete consequences:
- Line 729 (run_workflow_inner replay_capture setup): set_decision_capture runs under an orphan cx, so a parent cancellation cannot propagate through the capture adapter.
- Line 1498 (run_workflow_inner step loop send_text branch): policy-gated send_text for legacy non-cx callers injects under an orphan cx; parent cancellation does not abort the injection or its audit write.
- Line 2021 (pub async fn run event loop child spawn): runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx is invoked with a fresh cx, orphaning the spawned workflow from the runtime's ambient cx — cancelling the ambient cx does NOT cancel the child workflow.

Real non-test callers hit these paths: mcp_tools.rs:2145 calls runner.run_workflow(...) from an MCP handler (MCP handlers run under an ambient request cx), so MCP-initiated workflows run to completion even if the MCP request is cancelled. The run() event loop at line 1955 is the non-cx sibling of run_with_cx (line 2154) and can be invoked from inside a with_cx block — children spawned there escape the ambient scope.

Fix: replace each 'let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();' with the ambient-inheriting pattern 'let request_cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);'. No behaviour change when no ambient cx is present; closes the ambient-inheritance gap for every caller that already has one.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T22:07:15.266712Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T22:12:37.792231Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T22:12:37.792041Z","close_reason":"Completed: replaced 3 stale for_request() fallbacks with Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request) in runner.rs (replay_capture setup, step-loop send_text, run() event-loop child spawn). cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib passes clean. Commit 09014aae.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-sz7lc","title":"[golden] dogfood corpus metadata freezes wall-clock captured_at timestamps","description":"Dogfood corpus metadata currently stores literal wall-clock capture times in files like crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/codex/device_code_dogfood_2026_04_21_macos.meta.json:4 and crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/claude_code/compaction_complete_dogfood_2026_04_21_macos.meta.json:4. pattern_corpus.rs:88-98 only validates ISO-8601 shape, so these exact timestamps are not semantically asserted but they are still byte-frozen golden artifacts. Similar to ft-55l4f, this couples fixture churn to incidental capture time rather than behavior.\n\nRisk: future dogfood refreshes generate noisy golden diffs for pure recapture events; reviewers cannot distinguish behavioral drift from wall-clock drift.\n\nFix direction: canonicalize dogfood metadata for stability (for example replace captured_at with a placeholder or move the exact acquisition timestamp out of the golden-asserted corpus path), while preserving any provenance needed for humans elsewhere. Keep pattern-corpus semantic checks focused on scenario/source/platform/sanitized/cross_platform, not literal capture clock time.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:11:54.368499Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:17:57.689450Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-sz7lc","title":"[golden] dogfood corpus metadata freezes wall-clock captured_at timestamps","description":"Dogfood corpus metadata currently stores literal wall-clock capture times in files like crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/codex/device_code_dogfood_2026_04_21_macos.meta.json:4 and crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/claude_code/compaction_complete_dogfood_2026_04_21_macos.meta.json:4. pattern_corpus.rs:88-98 only validates ISO-8601 shape, so these exact timestamps are not semantically asserted but they are still byte-frozen golden artifacts. Similar to ft-55l4f, this couples fixture churn to incidental capture time rather than behavior.\n\nRisk: future dogfood refreshes generate noisy golden diffs for pure recapture events; reviewers cannot distinguish behavioral drift from wall-clock drift.\n\nFix direction: canonicalize dogfood metadata for stability (for example replace captured_at with a placeholder or move the exact acquisition timestamp out of the golden-asserted corpus path), while preserving any provenance needed for humans elsewhere. Keep pattern-corpus semantic checks focused on scenario/source/platform/sanitized/cross_platform, not literal capture clock time.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:11:54.368499Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:19:54.810656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:19:54.810409Z","close_reason":"Fixed in e4719ebc. All 8 <name>_dogfood_*.meta.json files now use canonical captured_at='1970-01-01T00:00:00Z'. README Dogfood-metadata section documents the convention: real acquisition date lives in the filename (_dogfood_YYYY_MM_DD_*) and notes field; captured_at is a byte-frozen placeholder only. pattern_corpus.rs at :88-98 only validates ISO-8601 shape so no test code change needed — epoch zero still parses. Eliminates wall-clock churn in dogfood-recapture diffs so reviewers can distinguish behavioural drift from clock drift.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-szs5k","title":"[compile] RecvError::Cancelled rollout leaves non-exhaustive matches in event stream and workflow runner","description":"Pane 3's runtime_compat rollout added RecvError::Cancelled at crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs:666, but downstream matches still only handle Lagged/Closed. Current compile-stop evidence from cargo-local --no-run lands at exact sites:\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs:574\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs:586\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs:749\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs:786\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs:1952\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs:2167\n\nThis leaves frankenterm-core test targets uncompilable until each match handles Err(events::RecvError::Cancelled) explicitly.  {"id":"ft-szuzd","title":"mcp_tools: schema-declared numeric maxima on wa.cass_* and wa.wait_for/wa.send are not enforced by handlers","description":"Several wa.* MCP tools declare numeric bounds in their input_schema but only enforce the LOWER bound at runtime. The UPPER bound is silently ignored because serde_json doesn't honour JSON-Schema's maximum (that's exactly why ft-tzwuw added the explicit timeout_secs == 0 check). Same shape applies to the maximum side:

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:526 wa.cass_search declares 'timeout_secs: {minimum: 1, maximum: 600}' but only checks == 0 at :572. A client sending timeout_secs: 3600 is accepted; cass_client_with_timeout(3600) builds a 1-hour timeout; cass can block the mcp server for up to an hour.
- Same file wa.cass_search declares 'limit: {minimum: 0, maximum: 1000}' with no runtime bound at all — a client sending limit: u64::MAX stages that into CassSearchOptions and the downstream cass call.
- wa.cass_view (:657), wa.cass_expand (:763), wa.wait_for (:1202-area), wa.send (:1926-area) repeat the same shape for timeout_secs.

wa.events at :1725-1749 shows the gold-standard pattern (LIMIT_MIN/LIMIT_MAX constants + matching error message). Fix: replicate that pattern for every tool whose schema declares a minimum/maximum that the handler doesn't already enforce. Scope this bead to wa.cass_search first (both bounds); sibling tools follow-on with pointers to this bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:57:22.700960Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:00:36.248624Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:00:36.248425Z","close_reason":"Landed in 4dc2d752 — 132 lines of mcp_tools.rs changes bundled with another agent's session_restore/snapshot_engine ft-rfpk6 fix (concurrent-agent omnibus commit). The bundled changes are exactly the wa.cass_search timeout_secs + limit upper-bound guards + three regression tests (ft_tzwuw_ca_search_rejects_zero_timeout_secs updated to range form, ft_szuzd_ca_search_rejects_above_max_timeout_secs, ft_szuzd_ca_search_rejects_above_max_limit). Parallel gaps in wa.cass_view/wa.cass_expand/wa.wait_for/wa.send remain — follow-on sweeps can mirror this same pattern.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
